seo.title: Managed Asset Search | Neel
seo.description: An information architecture case study on improving enterprise asset discovery through metadata design, taxonomy, and clearer search structure.

sections.title: Managed Asset Search
summary: This project focuses on making complex asset libraries easier to search, understand, and retrieve.
The work centers on metadata structure, taxonomy decisions, and a clearer information architecture for discovery.

media.alt: Animated interface preview for Managed Asset Search.

items.label: Problem
items.value: Large asset libraries become hard to navigate when metadata is inconsistent and category structures are unclear.

items.label: Approach
items.value: Used information architecture and taxonomy design to make search paths more legible and retrieval more predictable.

items.label: Outcome
items.value: The concept improves findability by making structure, labels, and content relationships easier to understand.

notes.title: Discovery gap
notes.text: Search quality often breaks long before the search bar itself, at the level of naming and structure.

notes.title: Metadata first
notes.text: Better taxonomy decisions made the interface feel clearer without adding more visual complexity.

notes.title: System payoff
notes.text: The real value came from making retrieval more dependable across teams and content types.

sections.label: Context
sections.heading: Search gets easier when the structure behind it carries more of the cognitive load
sections.aside: The challenge was not just improving an interface, but clarifying the system underneath it. Metadata patterns, labels, and hierarchy had to work together so users could find assets with less guesswork.

cards.title:
Audit
Content Patterns
cards.description: The work started by identifying repeated labeling issues, metadata gaps, and retrieval blockers across the system.

cards.title:
Reshape
Taxonomy
cards.description: Categories and terms were restructured to create a more stable mental model for how assets are organized.

cards.title:
Improve
Findability
cards.description: The interface then surfaced that structure more clearly so search felt faster, calmer, and more dependable.

image.alt: Interface preview for Managed Asset Search.

metrics.label: Structure
metrics.text: Taxonomy decisions create a more stable discovery model.

metrics.label: Search
metrics.text: Users get to relevant assets with less ambiguity.

metrics.label: Metadata
metrics.text: Classification becomes more consistent across the system.

metrics.label: Retrieval
metrics.text: Asset discovery feels more predictable across teams.
